meta.title: Wave 2 — OOO + OMX Absorption Pass
meta.description: Full Ouroboros + oh-my-codex feature pass. Classify, map, absorb. Follows Wave 1 AXI pattern.

props.title: OOO + OMX Full Pass
props.subtitle: 21 features across Ouroboros (12) and oh-my-codex (9). Classify what Lev already has, absorb the patterns we don't.

props.content: Wave 1 absorbed AXI patterns into /ux and /tribunal. Wave 2 does the same for Ouroboros (the spec-first workflow engine) and oh-my-codex (the Codex orchestration runtime). These are the two most architecturally similar projects to Lev — OOO converges on the same primitives from a different direction, OMX ships the tmux runtime we need. Most of what they have, Lev already has. This pass surfaces the gaps and maps the absorptions.

props.label: STATUS BREAKDOWN
props.title: What We Already Have vs What's Missing
✅ Implemented (6)
props.content:
**Lev already has equivalents — no absorption needed, just validate parity.**

- ooo-02 Immutable seed spec → `dna/*.yaml` + `.lev/pm/specs/`
- ooo-04 3-stage eval gate → `core/orchestration/src/eval/types.ts`
- ooo-07 PAL Router → `core/harness/` adapter selection
- ooo-08 Ralph loop → `plugins/core-sdlc/flows/lev-ralph.flow.yaml`
- ooo-09 Event sourcing → `core/event-bus/` + LevEvent JSONL
- omx-04 Ralph loop → same as ooo-08
- omx-08 Role prompts → `~/.claude/skills/` + plugins

props.title: 🔀 Partial (11)
props.content:
**Lev has the primitive, OOO/OMX has the refinement. Absorb the refinement.**

- Ambiguity scoring (ooo-01, omx-02)
- Double Diamond (ooo-03)
- Ontology convergence (ooo-05)
- 9 personas (ooo-06)
- Drift measurement (ooo-10)
- Brownfield detection (ooo-11)
- 4-lane model (omx-01)
- Tmux team lifecycle (omx-03)
- RALPLAN consensus (omx-05)
- Auto-nudge (omx-09)

props.title: ❌ Missing (4)
props.content:
**Lev has no equivalent. Build, reject, or absorb partially.**

- ooo-12 TUI dashboard → deferred to lev-tmux Rust crate (Wave 3)
- omx-06 Machine API (programmatic mode control) → needs design
- omx-07 HUD status display → deferred to lev-tmux
- omx-03 Tmux team lifecycle → this IS the tmux-control-plane workstream

props.label: OUROBOROS PASS
props.title: 12 Features × Absorption Map
props.subtitle: OOO is architecturally closest to Lev. Its Socratic interview + ambiguity gate + ontology convergence are the refinement layer on top of what Lev already has.

props.content:
The key OOO insight: **ambiguity is measurable**. OOO scores alignment on 5 dimensions (intent, outcome, scope, constraints, success) with a mathematical threshold (≤0.2) before allowing work to proceed. Lev has `interview` and `cdo` but doesn't gate on a score. That's the absorption target.

The second insight: **ontology convergence as exit condition**. OOO's evolutionary loop runs until the ontology similarity between iterations hits ≥0.95 — which is a deterministic stopping criterion. Lev's loops stop on `--until` semantic completion, which is regex-based and fragile. Absorbing convergence math gives us deterministic exit.

props.title: 🧭 → /interview
props.content:
**Absorb:** Ambiguity scoring formula

`ambiguity = 1 - sum(clarity_i × weight_i)`
weights: intent 30%, outcome 25%, scope 20%, constraints 15%, success 10%
Gate: score ≤ 0.2 before proceeding

**Also absorb:** Double Diamond phases (Discover → Define → Design → Deliver) as the default interview flow structure.

**Section to add:** 'Mathematical Ambiguity Gate (from OOO + OMX)'

props.title: ✅ → /validate + eval-harness
props.content:
**Absorb:** Ontology convergence as stopping criterion

`similarity = 0.5 × name_overlap + 0.3 × type_match + 0.2 × exact_match`
Exit when ≥ 0.95 OR max_iterations reached

**Section to add to eval-harness.flow.yaml:** 'Ontology Convergence Exit (from OOO)'

**Why:** Replaces fragile regex-based --until with deterministic math.

props.title: 🎯 → /lev-align
props.content:
**Absorb:** Composite drift measurement

`drift = 0.5 × goal_drift + 0.3 × constraint_drift + 0.2 × ontology_drift`

**Section to add:** 'Weighted Drift Composite (from OOO)'

**Why:** Lev has per-gate drift but no unified composite score. OOO's formula gives one number to watch.

props.title: 🔍 → /prior-art + /cdo
props.content:
**Absorb into /prior-art:** Brownfield auto-detection patterns — scan repo structure, detect conventions, surface deviations.

**Absorb into /cdo:** Nine personas as optional expansion roster (Socratic/Ontologist/Contrarian/Systemsist/Devil's-Advocate/Synthesizer/etc.) — CDO can invoke by name when tribunal is too wide.

**Already covered by tribunal/cdo/adversarial** — just document the bridge.

props.label: OMX PASS
props.title: 9 Features × Absorption Map
props.subtitle: OMX is the tmux runtime + 4-lane operator model. Most tmux lifecycle maps to the lev-tmux Rust crate (Wave 3). The lane model + deep-interview patterns absorb now.

props.content:
OMX is split across two planes: **(1) operator UX** (4-lane model, deep-interview, role prompts, RALPLAN) — this is what we absorb now into skills. **(2) runtime infrastructure** (tmux team lifecycle, machine API, HUD, auto-nudge) — this belongs in lev-tmux Wave 3, not skill absorption.

The 4-lane model IS the Lev taxonomy v1 already. OMX's deep-interview skill is the working production implementation of OOO's ambiguity scoring (same formula, verified live at .worktrees/lev-omx/.omx/state/). RALPLAN consensus is CDO by another name.

rows.feature: Tmux team lifecycle (N workers, shared task list, commit ledger)
Ralph persistent loop with architect verification

props.title: 🧭 → /interview (shared with OOO)
props.content:
**Absorb:** Production-verified ambiguity scoring formula

From `.worktrees/lev-omx/.codex/skills/deep-interview/`. Same formula as OOO but with live state proof:
- Round 1 ambiguity: 0.2455 (above threshold)
- Round 2 ambiguity: 0.159 (below threshold, gate passed)
- Dimensions scored: intent, outcome, scope, constraints, success, brownfield context

**Single absorption serves both OOO and OMX.**

props.title: 📝 → /cdo + /lev-plan
props.content:
**Absorb:** RALPLAN consensus pattern

Structured deliberation flow: proposal → 3 model perspectives → consensus score → commit or iterate.

**Section to add to /cdo:** 'RALPLAN Consensus Pattern (from OMX)'
**Section to add to /lev-plan:** 'Consensus Gate Before Plan Commit'

**Already close to CDO's team mode** — the absorption is mostly naming + exit criteria.

props.title: 🔄 → /autodev-lev + /cdo roster
props.content:
**Absorb into /autodev-lev:** Auto-nudge heuristics — when to interrupt agents, stall detection, proceed-intent signals (OMX has these working in production).

**Absorb into /cdo:** 20-role OMX roster as optional personas:
analyst, architect, build-fixer, code-reviewer, code-simplifier, critic, debugger, dependency-expert, designer, executor, explore, git-master, planner, researcher, security-reviewer, team-executor, test-engineer, verifier, vision, writer

props.label: WHAT'S NEW
props.title: Wave 2 Delta — Net Gains for Lev

props.content:
**Net new to Lev after Wave 2 absorption:**

1. **Mathematical ambiguity gate** — not just Socratic interview, but a score with a threshold. Before: subjective. After: ambiguity ≤ 0.2 to proceed.
2. **Ontology convergence exit** — not just --until regex, but `similarity ≥ 0.95` as deterministic stopping criterion.
3. **Composite drift score** — one number combining goal/constraint/ontology drift weighted 50/30/20.
4. **Named 20-role roster in /cdo** — tribunal was generic 3-model; now CDO can invoke specific roles by name.
5. **Brownfield detection in /prior-art** — scan for repo conventions, surface deviations as evidence.
6. **RALPLAN consensus pattern** — proposal → 3-perspective → consensus → commit/iterate.
7. **Auto-nudge heuristics in /autodev-lev** — production-tested stall detection from OMX live state.

rows.pattern: Double Diamond decomposition (Discover → Define → Design → Deliver)
Immutable seed spec from interview output

props.title: 1. Edit skill docs
props.content:
Add absorption sections to 8 Lev skills:

- /interview (ambiguity gate)
- /work (Double Diamond + 4 lanes)
- /validate + eval-harness (convergence exit)
- /lev-align (drift composite)
- /cdo (personas + RALPLAN + roster)
- /prior-art (brownfield detection)
- /lev-plan (crystallization + consensus gate)
- /autodev-lev (auto-nudge heuristics)

props.title: 2. Update parity YAMLs
props.content:
Mark absorbed features with `action: absorbed` + `absorbed_into: /skill-name`. Schema still frozen through 2026-04-17, but these are non-breaking field additions.

Update `.lev/pm/parity/ouroboros.yaml` and `.lev/pm/parity/omx.yaml`.

props.title: 3. Defer missing to Wave 3
props.content:
Wave 3 = lev-tmux Rust crate. Handles:
- omx-03 Tmux team lifecycle
- omx-06 Machine API
- omx-07 HUD status display
- ooo-12 TUI dashboard

All four are infrastructure, not skill absorptions.
